title: Scene Spec
description: The atomic creative unit. A scene is an EXPERIENCE unit, not a task unit. Experiential fields come first and are required; the plot advance (one_change) is small and optional — a scene whose one_change is empty is a legitimate pure-texture/relationship beat. This shape is what keeps scene-level writing from amplifying task-feeling.

emotional_temperature.description: Where the scene's feeling starts and where it moves.

weave_beats.description: Non-task texture beats (daily reaction, lived-in detail, relationship temperature). Budgeted first-class so texture is distributed, not squeezed out.

one_change.description: The single small thing that shifts. MAY be empty: a pure-texture scene advances nothing plot-wise.

enters_via.description: How any new information arrives. Must not be protagonist brain-summary or narrator explanation.

voice_exemplars.description: 2-3 real passages matching this scene's type, for voice transfer.

exit_on.description: External motion to cut on: action / arrival / interruption / cost / object — never 'objective achieved'.
